'''Ryan Shaw''' (born 26 September 1992) is an English former professional [[rugby league]] footballer who last played {{rlp|wg|ce}} or {{rlp|fb}} for the [[Barrow Raiders]] in [[RFL Championship|Championship]].

Shaw is a product of the [[Warrington Wolves]] academy, spending time on loan from [[Warrington Wolves|Warrington]] at the [[Castleford Tigers]] and the [[London Broncos]] in the [[Super League]], the [[Leigh Centurions]] and the [[Swinton Lions]] in the [[Co-operative Championship]], [[Barrow Raiders|Barrow]] in the [[League 1 (rugby league)|Championship 1]] and the [[Co-operative Championship]]. He played for the [[Bradford Bulls]] in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]], before signing for [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] in the top flight of English rugby league. He has also played for [[Leigh Centurions|Leigh]] on a permanent basis in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]].

==Background==
Shaw was born in [[Barrow-in-Furness]], [[Cumbria, England]].

==Career==
Shaw started his career in the academy at [[Warrington Wolves|Warrington]]. He had spells at [[Leigh Centurions|Leigh]] and [[Barrow Raiders|Barrow]] on dual-registration deals, but failed to make a first team appearance for Warrington. He was signed by [[Swinton Lions|Swinton]] for the 2013 season. He joined [[London Broncos]] on loan later that year, where he made his [[Super League]] début. He spent the 2014 season with hometown club, Barrow and rugby idol Mike Backhouse

===Bradford Bulls===
'''2015''' - [[2015 Bradford Bulls season|2015 Season]]

Shaw signed for Bradford on a one-year deal. He featured in the pre-season friendlies against [[Castleford Tigers]] and [[Leeds Rhinos|Leeds]]. He scored against [[Castleford Tigers]] (1 try) and [[Leeds Rhinos|Leeds]] (1 try).

He featured in Round 2 ([[Whitehaven R.L.F.C.|Whitehaven]]) to Round 23 ([[Halifax R.L.F.C.|Halifax]]) then in Qualifier 7 ([[Halifax R.L.F.C.|Halifax]]). He also featured in the [[Challenge Cup]] in Round 4 ([[Workington Town]]) to Round 5 ([[Hull Kingston Rovers]]). He scored against [[Featherstone Rovers]] (9 goals), [[Hunslet R.L.F.C.|Hunslet]] (4 tries, 16 goals), [[Batley Bulldogs|Batley]] (1 try, 8 goals), [[Workington Town]] (2 tries, 20 goals), [[Halifax R.L.F.C.|Halifax]] (4 tries, 13 goals), [[Dewsbury Rams]] (1 try, 6 goals), [[London Broncos]] (1 try, 10 goals), [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] (5 goals), [[Doncaster R.L.F.C.|Doncaster]] (2 tries, 9 goals), [[Whitehaven R.L.F.C.|Whitehaven]] (4 goals), [[Sheffield Eagles]] (1 try, 5 goals) and [[Leigh Centurions]] (6 goals).

===Hull KR===
Shaw left Bradford at the end of the 2015 season for Hull Kingston Rovers on the recommendation of former Great Britain prop [[Jamie Peacock]].{{citation needed|date=April 2023}}

===Leeds Tykes===
On 11 October 2019, it was reported that he had switched codes to rugby union and signed for [[Leeds Tykes]] in the [[RFU Championship]].<ref>{{cite web|website=Yorkshire Post|date=11 Oct 2019|accessdate=23 Jan 2021|title=Ryan Shaw ready for cross-code challenge after move from Hull KR to Yorkshire Carnegie|url=https://www.yorkshirepost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/ryan-shaw-ready-cross-code-challenge-after-move-hull-kr-yorkshire-carnegie-686081}}</ref>

===Leigh Centurions===
On 16 February 2020 it was reported that he had returned to rugby league and signed for [[Leigh Centurions|Leigh]] in the [[RFL Championship]].<ref>{{cite web|website=Hull Daily Mail|date=16 Feb 2020|accessdate=23 Jan 2021|title=Ex-Hull KR winger Ryan Shaw returns to rugby league after Yorkshire Carnegie exit|url=https://www.hulldailymail.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/rugby-league-news/ryan-shaw-yorkshire-carnegie-hull-3853962}}</ref>

===Barrow Raiders===
On 25 December 2020, it was reported that he had signed a two-year deal with [[Barrow Raiders|Barrow]] in the [[RFL League 1]].<ref>{{cite web|website=The Mail|date=25 Dec 2020|accessdate=23 Jan 2021|title=Former Super League player returns to his hometown club Barrow Raiders|url=https://www.nwemail.co.uk/sport/18968859.former-super-league-player-returns-hometown-club-barrow-raiders/}}</ref>
